heading: Tax-exempt status
label: 22507 Tax-exempt status
text: The personal property and funds of the corporation, whether principal or income, so long as held or used only to carry out the purposes of the corporation, are exempt from taxation by the United States Government, the District of Columbia, and the territories and possessions of the United States.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2250736:96 (1st sentence).(less proviso), .Feb. 24, 1925, ch. 303, § 643 Stat. 967The words “so long as held or used only to carry out the purposes of the corporation” are substituted for “held or used for the purposes hereof, pursuant to the provisions of this chapter . . . so long as the same shall be so used” to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “the United States Government, the District of Columbia, and the territories and possessions of the United States” are substituted for “the United States or any Territory or District thereof” for clarity.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2250736:96 (1st sentence).(less proviso), .Feb. 24, 1925, ch. 303, § 643 Stat. 967The words “so long as held or used only to carry out the purposes of the corporation” are substituted for “held or used for the purposes hereof, pursuant to the provisions of this chapter . . . so long as the same shall be so used” to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “the United States Government, the District of Columbia, and the territories and possessions of the United States” are substituted for “the United States or any Territory or District thereof” for clarity.
